comment: Pydantic models used extensively for requests/responses, e.g., src/api/courses/schemas.py CourseFullDTO with Field(examples), routers use response_model=list[CourseShortDTO]
Alembic migrations configured, alembic/versions/ multiple files like d21fb58adff2_init_db.py, env.py imports all models from src.infrastructure.sqlalchemy.*.models
Global handlers in src/app.py add_exception_handler for ApplicationError and IncorrectUUIDError returning JSONResponse(ErrorResponse), used in routers
Depends used everywhere, e.g., src/api/admin/course_run/dependencies.py get_admin_course_run_command_service(db_session=Depends(get_async_session))
CORSMiddleware added in src/app.py add_cors(application.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=['*'], ...))
Pagination in src/api/base_pagination.py Paginator, used in src/api/courses/router.py get_courses with Paginator(data=courses, page_size=9), CoursesPaginationResponse
Ruff configured in pyproject.toml [tool.ruff] with lint.select=['ALL'], ignore list, fix=true; readme.md poetry run ruff check src
unit_tests/domain/*/*.py and integration_tests/*/*.py with tests, conftest.py fixtures
Pydantic Fields with examples, e.g., src/api/auth/schemas.py AuthTokenResponse(auth_token=Field('423fsdf23ffs3a2sd3432sd2fa2fag'))
src/infrastructure/security/password_service.py create_hashed_password with pbkdf2_hmac, used in auth command_service.register_talent
Repositories like src/domain/courses/course_repository.py ICourseRepository, implemented in src/infrastructure/sqlalchemy/courses/repository.py
CORS in src/app.py CORSMiddleware(allow_origins=['*'], allow_credentials=True, allow_methods=['*'], allow_headers=['*'])
No BackgroundTasks or Celery in routers/services; all operations synchronous in endpoints
No print statements; no import logging or logging calls in src/ code
Functions like create_course_run(course_id: str, season: str, year: int) -> str in src/services/course_run/command_service.py descriptive
All public methods annotated, e.g., src/services/courses/command_service.py async def create_course(self, name_: str) -> str
No global mutables; state in repos/services like self.session in SQLAlchemy repos
No generators/yield for sequences; lists loaded fully e.g., await repo.get_all()
No redundant comparisons like if is_valid == True; conditions direct e.g., if user.role.value != 'admin'
Comprehensions used e.g., src/api/courses/router.py [CourseShortDTO.from_domain(course) for course in paginator.get_data_by_page(page)]
Constants like src/domain/courses/constants.py PERIODS, ROLES; used in value_objects e.g., if value not in PERIODS
Conditions simple; no complex broken into booleans, but no violations
No mutable defaults like list=[]; uses | None = None patterns not seen but no violations
No custom decorators; repeated error handling manual in routers, DI via Depends
integration_tests/conftest.py fixtures test_async_session, test_cache_session; unit_tests/domain/auth/test_value_objects.py @pytest.mark.parametrize
Async with asyncpg/async_redis/SQLAlchemy asyncsession; no sync blocking like time.sleep/requests
No asyncio.gather/create_task; no parallel async tasks
No asyncio.timeout or client timeouts visible in DB/Redis/HTTP calls
src/config.py loads POSTGRES_PASSWORD etc. from .env; .test.env example; no hardcodes
Dockerfile.production present in root
dev.docker-compose.yaml and test.docker-compose.yaml for postgres/redis
Modular: src/api/domain/infrastructure/services; routers/schemas per module
Business logic in src/services/*/*.py command_service/query_service; routers depend on services e.g., command_service.create_course
